key_findings: BREAKTHROUGH: ID=0.898 - pure-declarative YAML is MORE compact than original BSV
YAML only describes structure (WHAT): interfaces, ports, sub-modules, connections
LLM successfully inferred ALL behavioral logic from structural descriptions alone
AL=0.8 (L0/L1 pure declarative) - best abstraction level achieved so far
LIS=0 maintained - all language-specific info properly scoped
This validates the core hypothesis: graph structure + natural language intent → working RTL
CONCERN: This approach may not scale to complex algorithmic logic (arbitration, state machines)
next_actions: iter_004: Test pure-declarative approach on a module with complex algorithmic logic
iter_004: Try Arbitration.bsv or WorkCompGen.bsv to stress-test LLM inference capability
iter_004: Consider hybrid: structural YAML + minimal algorithmic hints for complex logic
